title: Vermont: data-broker registry, but no general deletion right
summary: Vermont's comprehensive privacy bill was passed but vetoed, so there is no general state deletion right. Vermont does, however, run a data-broker registry: brokers operating there must register, and the public registry can help you find who holds your data and how to opt out. For removal, use the data broker's own opt-out (below).
disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed. The registry helps you locate brokers; it does not itself delete your data — verify before relying on this.
